steps.title: Find line k's slope
steps.content:
From the graph, line $k$ passes through $(0,4)$ and $(3,0)$. Its slope is
$$\frac{0-4}{3-0}=-\frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Use perpendicular slopes
steps.content: Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals. Since line $k$ has slope $-\frac{4}{3}$, line $j$ has slope $\frac{3}{4}$.

steps.title: Use the given point
steps.content:
Substitute slope $\frac{3}{4}$ and point $(-20,-23)$ into point-slope form, then simplify.
$$y+23=\frac{3}{4}(x+20)$$
$$y=\frac{3}{4}x+15-23$$
$$y=\frac{3}{4}x-8$$
The answer is A.
